title: drypetes cumingii
intro: Drypetes cumingii, also known as the Philippine copperleaf or copper-leaf tree, is a species of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that is native to the Philippines and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. Drypetes cumingii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used to make a decoction or tincture for medicinal purposes.
